task_completion.description: Verify that Tor Browser is open and navigated to the local chart tool.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Tor Browser is visible with 'file:///home/ga/Documents/OfflineTools/chart_tool.html' in the address bar.

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent triggers the canvas extraction warning in Tor Browser.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A prompt or icon in the Tor Browser address bar or viewport indicating an attempt to extract HTML5 canvas image data.

task_completion.description: Verify that a permanent exception is granted for canvas extraction.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Tor Browser permissions popup shows canvas extraction is allowed with a 'Remember my decision' or 'Always Allow' option selected, or the Page Info/Settings shows the permission explicitly allowed for the local file.

task_completion.description: Verify that the chart data is exported as a Base64 string in the tool.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The chart tool UI displays a long Base64 string (often starting with 'data:image/png;base64,') after the permission is granted.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Base64 string is saved to the correct text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal shows the Base64 string being saved to '/home/ga/Documents/OfflineTools/exported_chart.txt'. The string should appear long and dense (representing >1000 bytes of data).

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Tor Browser GUI to grant permissions.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Tor Browser UI and permission popups, rather than terminal commands modifying SQLite permission databases directly.

integrity.description: Verify the saved data is actual Base64 image data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The content saved to the text file is a dense, continuous block of alphanumeric characters typical of Base64, not a short placeholder or manually typed string.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with a real text editor or terminal to save the file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file '/home/ga/Documents/OfflineTools/exported_chart.txt' is seen being written to in a text editor (e.g., gedit, nano) or via standard output redirection in the terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The local HTML tool is located at /home/ga/Documents/OfflineTools/chart_tool.html. The expected output file is /home/ga/Documents/OfflineTools/exported_chart.txt. The extracted Base64 string must be larger than 1000 bytes. The permission granted in Tor Browser must be for 'canvas/extractData'.
